content.question: Would eliminating or delaying the 6- and 8-year tracks reduce segregation and inequality if school choice, catchment rules, and local policy-driven segregation remain unchanged?
content.reduction_path: Pilot de-tracking reform in matched local education authorities while holding school choice and catchment policies constant. Compare segregation and achievement outcomes to control areas. Separately test scenarios combining de-tracking with and without school choice restrictions to isolate track system contribution.
